title: [lichen_l2.c:547] last_seen updated even if signature verification fails on second+ match
description: In peer_try_all_pubkeys constant-time iteration, when found_idx >= 0 (line 545), last_seen is updated at line 547. But the constant-time loop continues and may overwrite ctx state. If a SECOND peer's pubkey also verifies (which shouldn't happen normally but could with key reuse or bugs), the code at line 524-528 only updates found_idx if found_idx < 0 (first match). So last_seen is updated for the first match only, but the overwrite of found_idx is prevented. This is correct but subtle - the code handles multi-match correctly by using first match, but a code reader might think last_seen could be updated multiple times.
close_reason: Fixed: Added clarifying comment explaining last_seen is only updated AFTER the loop completes for the first matching peer
